summary: The test suite is solid and purposeful. All five public functions are exercised, every major requirement from the task description has at least one test, and all data is mocked — no live system calls. Filtering logic is tested for CPU-only, memory-only, and OR-combined thresholds; top-N is validated for both sort axes and for the over-request edge case; null CPU handling is explicitly covered; and the error-handling Describe block validates mandatory-parameter enforcement and invalid enum rejection. Gaps that prevent a 5: (1) Get-ProcessInfo is tested only via direct parameter passing, never via the pipeline path declared with ValueFromPipeline; (2) report-format testing is purely string-match/presence — column alignment, header labels, and separator lines are never asserted, so a reformatted report would pass silently; (3) Invoke-ProcessMonitor has no test exercising a memory-only breach (all orchestration tests trigger CPU); (4) boundary conditions like a single-element input list, zero-length ProcessData, or the exact rounding of WorkingSet64 values are not covered. BeforeAll scoping, Arrange/Act/Assert comments, and descriptive It-block names make the suite readable and maintainable. Overall a trustworthy baseline that would catch the majority of regressions.
